break the ice.example: A joke can help break the ice at a party.

piece of cake.definition: 小菜一碟；轻而易举的事
piece of cake.example: The exam was a piece of cake for her.

hit the nail on the head.example: You hit the nail on the head with that analysis.

once in a blue moon.example: We only see each other once in a blue moon.

under the weather.example: I'm feeling a bit under the weather today.

cost an arm and a leg.phrase: cost an arm and a leg

the ball is in your court.phrase: the ball is in your court
the ball is in your court.definition: 该你做决定了；轮到你采取行动了
the ball is in your court.example: I've made my offer — the ball is in your court now.

bite the bullet.example: I decided to bite the bullet and have the surgery.

a blessing in disguise.definition: 塞翁失马，焉知非福；因祸得福

burn the midnight oil.definition: 开夜车；熬夜工作或学习

get out of hand.example: The situation quickly got out of hand.

go the extra mile.example: She always goes the extra mile for her students.

hang in there.example: Hang in there — things will get better soon.

it takes two to tango.definition: 一个巴掌拍不响；双方都有责任
it takes two to tango.example: Don't just blame him — it takes two to tango.

kill two birds with one stone.phrase: kill two birds with one stone
kill two birds with one stone.example: By cycling to work, I kill two birds with one stone — I save money and stay fit.

let the cat out of the bag.phrase: let the cat out of the bag

miss the boat.example: If you don't apply now, you'll miss the boat.

no pain no gain.definition: 不劳无获；没有付出就没有收获

on the same page.example: We need to make sure everyone is on the same page.

see eye to eye.example: We don't always see eye to eye on politics.

speak of the devil.example: Speak of the devil — here comes Tom now!

spill the beans.example: Come on, spill the beans — what happened?

the best of both worlds.definition: 两全其美；兼得两者之长

the tip of the iceberg.example: These complaints are just the tip of the iceberg.

throw in the towel.example: After years of trying, he finally threw in the towel.

time flies.definition: 光阴似箭；时间过得真快
time flies.example: Time flies when you're having fun.

turn a blind eye.definition: 视而不见；睁一只眼闭一只眼
turn a blind eye.example: The manager turned a blind eye to the problem.

when pigs fly.definition: 不可能的事；太阳打西边出来
when pigs fly.example: He'll clean his room when pigs fly.

wrap your head around.example: I can't wrap my head around this concept.

actions speak louder than words.definition: 行胜于言；事实胜于雄辩
actions speak louder than words.example: Don't just promise — actions speak louder than words.

add insult to injury.example: To add insult to injury, it started raining.

at the drop of a hat.phrase: at the drop of a hat
at the drop of a hat.example: She'd travel anywhere at the drop of a hat.

back to square one.example: The deal fell through, so we're back to square one.

barking up the wrong tree.definition: 找错了对象；搞错了方向
barking up the wrong tree.example: If you think I did it, you're barking up the wrong tree.

beat around the bush.example: Stop beating around the bush and tell me the truth.

better late than never.definition: 迟做总比不做好；亡羊补牢

by the skin of your teeth.phrase: by the skin of your teeth
by the skin of your teeth.example: I passed the exam by the skin of my teeth.

cut corners.example: Don't cut corners on safety procedures.

every cloud has a silver lining.phrase: every cloud has a silver lining
every cloud has a silver lining.definition: 黑暗中总有一线光明；否极泰来

get cold feet.example: She got cold feet and cancelled the wedding.

give the benefit of the doubt.phrase: give the benefit of the doubt
give the benefit of the doubt.definition: 姑且相信；不作有罪推定

in hot water.example: He's in hot water for missing the deadline.

jump on the bandwagon.example: Many companies jumped on the AI bandwagon.

keep your chin up.example: Keep your chin up — you'll find a new job soon.

learn the ropes.example: It took me a few weeks to learn the ropes at the new job.

make ends meet.definition: 勉强维持生计；收支相抵

nip it in the bud.definition: 防患于未然；把问题消灭在萌芽中

off the top of my head.phrase: off the top of my head
off the top of my head.example: Off the top of my head, I'd say about fifty people came.

once bitten, twice shy.definition: 一朝被蛇咬，十年怕井绳
once bitten, twice shy.example: After that bad investment, I'm once bitten, twice shy.

put all your eggs in one basket.phrase: put all your eggs in one basket
put all your eggs in one basket.definition: 孤注一掷；把所有希望寄托于一处
put all your eggs in one basket.example: Don't put all your eggs in one basket — diversify your investments.

read between the lines.definition: 领会言外之意；读懂弦外之音
read between the lines.example: If you read between the lines, his letter is quite critical.

ring a bell.example: That name rings a bell, but I can't place it.

sit on the fence.example: You can't sit on the fence forever — make a decision.

take it with a grain of salt.phrase: take it with a grain of salt
take it with a grain of salt.example: Take his advice with a grain of salt.

the last straw.definition: 最后一根稻草；忍无可忍
the last straw.example: That remark was the last straw — I quit.

under no circumstances.definition: 在任何情况下都不；绝对不
under no circumstances.example: Under no circumstances should you open that door.

up in the air.example: Our holiday plans are still up in the air.

whole nine yards.example: She went the whole nine yards for the party.

a drop in the ocean.example: A thousand pounds is just a drop in the ocean compared to what we need.

play it by ear.example: We don't have a plan — let's just play it by ear.

a penny for your thoughts.definition: 你在想什么？（问对方在想什么）

the elephant in the room.definition: 显而易见却被忽视的问题
the elephant in the room.example: Nobody wanted to mention the elephant in the room.

food for thought.definition: 发人深省的事；值得思考的问题
food for thought.example: The lecture gave us plenty of food for thought.

in a nutshell.example: In a nutshell, we need more funding.

keep an eye on.example: Could you keep an eye on the children for me?

think outside the box.definition: 跳出固有思维；创新思考

a double-edged sword.example: Social media is a double-edged sword.

the bottom line.definition: 最重要的一点；关键所在

at the end of the day.phrase: at the end of the day

pros and cons.example: Let's weigh the pros and cons before deciding.

as a result.example: He didn't study hard. As a result, he failed the exam.

in terms of.example: In terms of quality, this product is the best.

in addition to.example: In addition to English, she speaks French and German.

for the most part.example: For the most part, the students were well-behaved.

on the whole.example: On the whole, I think the plan is good.

for instance.example: There are many options — for instance, we could try online advertising.

in contrast.example: The north is dry. In contrast, the south gets lots of rain.

in conclusion.example: In conclusion, more research is needed in this area.

sum up.example: To sum up, we need to act quickly.

it should be noted that.example: It should be noted that these results are preliminary.

there is growing evidence that.definition: 有越来越多的证据表明

from a practical standpoint.example: From a practical standpoint, the plan is not feasible.

from a different perspective.example: Let's look at the issue from a different perspective.

on a global scale.example: Climate change is a problem on a global scale.

keep in mind.example: Keep in mind that deadlines are strict.

bear in mind.example: Bear in mind that prices may change.

from scratch.example: She built the business from scratch.

keep track of.example: It's hard to keep track of all the changes.

take a toll on.example: Stress takes a toll on your health.

take the lead.example: China has taken the lead in renewable energy investment.

the other way around.example: It's not that I don't like him — it's the other way around.

tip of the iceberg.example: What we've seen so far is just the tip of the iceberg.

to the point.example: Her comments were brief and to the point.

trial and error.example: We learned through trial and error.

turn of events.example: In a surprising turn of events, the underdog won.

ups and downs.example: Every relationship has its ups and downs.

water under the bridge.example: We had our disagreements, but that's water under the bridge.

way of life.example: Technology has changed our way of life.

well-being.example: The government should prioritise the well-being of its citizens.

with flying colours.example: She passed the exam with flying colours.

a vicious circle.example: Poverty and poor education create a vicious circle.

according to.example: According to the report, crime rates have decreased.

after all.example: Don't be too hard on her — she's only a child, after all.

all the more.example: His criticism made her all the more determined.

amount to.example: The total cost amounted to several million dollars.

apart from.example: Apart from the weather, the trip was wonderful.

around the clock.example: Doctors worked around the clock to save patients.

as a rule.example: As a rule, I go to bed before midnight.

as it were.example: He is, as it were, the backbone of the team.

at any rate.example: At any rate, we should try our best.

at large.example: The community at large will benefit from this project.

be aware of.example: You should be aware of the potential risks.

be likely to.example: Prices are likely to rise in the near future.

be well worth.example: The museum is well worth a visit.

bring to light.example: The investigation brought several issues to light.

by far.example: She is by far the most talented singer.

call for.example: The situation calls for immediate action.

call into question.example: The findings call into question the original hypothesis.

carry on.example: Please carry on with your work.

catch on.example: The trend quickly caught on among young people.

come about.example: How did this situation come about?

come down to.example: In the end, it all comes down to money.

dawn on.example: It dawned on me that I had been wrong.

derive from.example: Many English words derive from Latin.

every now and then.example: Every now and then, I like to treat myself to a nice dinner.

for good.example: She left the country for good.

from the outset.example: Problems were apparent from the outset.

get to grips with.example: It took me a while to get to grips with the software.

give way to.example: Traditional methods gave way to modern technology.

go beyond.example: This problem goes beyond what we expected.

have a tendency to.example: He has a tendency to exaggerate.

in a sense.example: In a sense, we are all responsible for the environment.

in any case.example: In any case, we should proceed as planned.

in the event of.example: In the event of a fire, use the emergency exits.

in this regard.example: The government has made progress in this regard.

in view of.example: In view of the circumstances, we decided to postpone.

lay the groundwork for.example: Early education lays the groundwork for lifelong learning.

lead to.example: Poor diet can lead to health problems.

let alone.example: He can't even cook rice, let alone a full meal.

little by little.example: Little by little, she improved her English.

more often than not.example: More often than not, simple solutions work best.

no matter what.example: I'll support you no matter what.

none the less.example: It was a difficult journey, but none the less rewarding.

on the one hand.example: On the one hand, I want to go; on the other, I'm tired.

other than.example: I have no choice other than to accept.

over and above.example: There are benefits over and above the salary.

point of view.example: Everyone has a different point of view.

put emphasis on.example: Schools should put more emphasis on creativity.

rest assured.example: Rest assured, we will solve the problem.

serve as.example: This example serves as a warning to others.

so far.example: So far, we have received 100 applications.

take action.example: It's time to take action against climate change.

take responsibility for.example: Everyone should take responsibility for their own actions.

the bulk of.example: The bulk of the funding goes to research.

the fact remains that.example: The fact remains that we have a long way to go.

the vast majority of.example: The vast majority of students passed the exam.

time and again.example: He has proved himself time and again.

to the extent that.example: She was helpful to the extent that she could be.

with this in mind.example: With this in mind, we revised our strategy.

above all.example: Above all, we must ensure the safety of the passengers.

alongside.example: She worked alongside her colleagues for years.

an array of.example: The store offers an array of organic products.

as such.example: He is the captain and, as such, must lead by example.

back and forth.example: We went back and forth on the issue for hours.

be at the heart of.example: Innovation is at the heart of the company's success.

be in keeping with.example: The design is in keeping with the building's heritage.

be of paramount importance.example: Safety is of paramount importance in the workplace.

be prone to.example: This area is prone to flooding.

bear witness to.example: The ruins bear witness to a once-great civilisation.

break new ground.definition: 开辟新天地；有创新突破
break new ground.example: The research breaks new ground in cancer treatment.

by definition.example: A democracy is, by definition, governed by the people.

catch up on.example: I need to catch up on some sleep.

close the gap.example: The government aims to close the gap between rich and poor.

come to a conclusion.example: After much discussion, we came to a conclusion.

contrary to.example: Contrary to popular belief, coffee is not always bad for you.

do more harm than good.example: Overprotective parenting can do more harm than good.

embark on.example: She embarked on a new career in teaching.

err on the side of caution.phrase: err on the side of caution
err on the side of caution.example: When in doubt, err on the side of caution.

get the hang of.example: It takes time to get the hang of a new language.

go a long way.example: A little kindness goes a long way.

have a bearing on.example: Your attitude has a direct bearing on your success.

in a position to.example: We are not in a position to comment at this time.

in pursuit of.example: Many people move abroad in pursuit of a better life.

in the interests of.example: In the interests of safety, please wear a seatbelt.

in this day and age.example: In this day and age, everyone should be able to access education.

keep abreast of.example: It's important to keep abreast of current affairs.

level the playing field.example: The new regulations aim to level the playing field.

loom large.example: Environmental concerns loom large in political debate.

make headway.example: We are finally making headway on the project.

mark a turning point.example: The discovery marked a turning point in science.

no wonder.example: You didn't sleep — no wonder you're tired.

of great significance.example: This finding is of great significance.

on the grounds of.example: She was dismissed on the grounds of misconduct.

one way or another.example: One way or another, we'll get it done.

owing to the fact that.example: He failed, owing to the fact that he didn't prepare.

play devil's advocate.example: Let me play devil's advocate for a moment.

provide insight into.example: The study provides insight into consumer behaviour.

reap the benefits.example: If you study hard, you will reap the benefits.

set the stage for.definition: 为……做好准备；为……奠定基础
set the stage for.example: This agreement sets the stage for future cooperation.

side by side.example: The two communities live side by side.

strike a balance.example: We need to strike a balance between work and leisure.

take a stand.example: It's time to take a stand against injustice.

take its toll.definition: 产生不良后果；造成损失
take its toll.example: Years of stress have taken their toll on his health.

the crux of the matter.example: The crux of the matter is funding.

think twice.example: You should think twice before making such a big decision.

to name but a few.example: There are many benefits — improved health, lower costs, and reduced pollution, to name but a few.

touch on.example: The speaker touched on several important issues.

turn the tide.example: New technology could turn the tide in the fight against disease.

uphold.example: It is our duty to uphold the law.

wreak havoc.example: The storm wreaked havoc on the coastal town.

at the outset.example: At the outset, I'd like to clarify a few points.

come to fruition.example: After years of planning, the project came to fruition.

in the foreseeable future.example: There's no sign of improvement in the foreseeable future.

on a broader level.example: On a broader level, the policy affects the entire economy.

at one's disposal.example: We have the latest technology at our disposal.

on the grounds.example: He was rejected on the grounds of insufficient experience.

turn a deaf ear to.example: The authorities turned a deaf ear to our complaints.

in stark contrast to.example: Her optimism was in stark contrast to his pessimism.

leave much to be desired.example: The quality of service leaves much to be desired.

to no avail.example: He tried to fix it, but to no avail.

the bottom line is.example: The bottom line is we need to cut costs.

at the core of.example: Education is at the core of national development.

a stepping stone.example: This job can be a stepping stone to a better career.

in a broader sense.example: In a broader sense, this issue affects all of society.

the driving force behind.example: Innovation is the driving force behind economic growth.

to that end.example: We want to improve efficiency. To that end, we've hired more staff.

go without saying.example: It goes without saying that hard work pays off.

have the upper hand.example: The home team had the upper hand throughout the match.

in the realm of.example: There have been breakthroughs in the realm of medicine.

make or break.example: This exam could make or break her future.

on the horizon.example: There are exciting changes on the horizon.

the root cause of.example: We must address the root cause of the problem.

a handful of.example: Only a handful of students got full marks.

go from strength to strength.example: The company has gone from strength to strength.

in no uncertain terms.example: She told him in no uncertain terms to leave.

a host of.example: The project faces a host of challenges.

above and beyond.example: She went above and beyond to help us.

at the mercy of.example: Farmers are at the mercy of the weather.

get a grip on.example: You need to get a grip on your spending.

in the pipeline.example: Several new products are in the pipeline.

on a par with.example: His work is on a par with the best in the field.

set a precedent.example: This ruling could set a dangerous precedent.

the lion's share.example: The lion's share of the budget goes to defence.

at this juncture.example: At this juncture, we must make a decision.

a bone of contention.example: Immigration has long been a bone of contention.

leave no stone unturned.definition: 想尽一切办法；不遗余力
leave no stone unturned.example: The police left no stone unturned in their investigation.
